item.title: Macrogenics Clinical Hold Lifted
item.description:
• Summary: The FDA has lifted a clinical hold on Macrogenics' oncology trial, allowing the company to resume development of its antibody-based cancer therapies, while Oxford and Bristol-Myers Squibb announce a separate collaboration in the field.
  • MoAs: N/A
  • Sources: Endpoints News, ApexOnco

item.title: Merck / TROP2
item.description:
• Summary: Merck is making a significant strategic bet on TROP2-targeting therapies, potentially including antibody-drug conjugates like sacituzumab govitecan. This move aims to expand Merck's oncology portfolio in areas such as breast and lung cancers.
  • MoAs: TROP2-targeting antibody-drug conjugates (e.g., sacituzumab govitecan) deliver cytotoxic payloads to cancer cells expressing TROP2, inducing cell death.
  • Sources: ApexOnco

item.title: Novartis-backed Sidewinder Therapeutics
item.description:
• Summary: Sidewinder Therapeutics, backed by Novartis, has raised $137 million in a Series B funding round to advance therapies for difficult-to-treat tumors. The funding supports the development of innovative oncology treatments targeting resistant cancers.
  • MoAs: N/A
  • Sources: BioSpace
